content.problemStatement:
Chef has two sequences of integers (A1,A2,тАж,AN)(A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_N)(A1тАЛ,A2тАЛ,тАж,ANтАЛ) and (B1,B2,тАж,BN)(B_1, B_2, \ldots, B_N)(B1тАЛ,B2тАЛ,тАж,BNтАЛ).

A sequence of integers is beautiful if it contains only distinct integers. For each valid iii:


Chef wants to create a beautiful sequence SiS_iSiтАЛ. Let's denote the length of this sequence by LiL_iLiтАЛ and the sequence by Si,1,Si,2,тАж,Si,LiS_{i,1}, S_{i,2}, \ldots, S_{i,L_i}Si,1тАЛ,Si,2тАЛ,тАж,Si,LiтАЛтАЛ.
The sequence SiS_iSiтАЛ is good if for each valid jjj, Si,jS_{i,j}Si,jтАЛ is either equal to Ai+jтИТ1A_{i+j-1}Ai+jтИТ1тАЛ or Bi+jтИТ1B_{i+j-1}Bi+jтИТ1тАЛ. Note that this means LiL_iLiтАЛ must be at most NтИТi+1N-i+1NтИТi+1.
Let's denote the maximum possible value of LiL_iLiтАЛ (the maximum value such that some good sequence SiS_iSiтАЛ with this length exists) by MiM_iMiтАЛ.
Help Chef find the number of good sequences SiS_iSiтАЛ that have the maximum possible length MiM_iMiтАЛ. Since this number may be enormous, compute it modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 5тЛЕ1055 \cdot 10^55тЛЕ105
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 5тЛЕ1035 \cdot 10^35тЛЕ103
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,тАж,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1тАЛ,A2тАЛ,тАж,ANтАЛ.
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers B1,B2,тАж,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1тАЛ,B2тАЛ,тАж,BNтАЛ.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing NNN space-separated integers. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these integers should be the number of possible sequences SiS_iSiтАЛ modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.
